description: no directory of presbytery churches, roll of presbytery or roll of candidates and licentiates of the presbytery churches is included,
no minutes of executive session
response: there was no executive session during the meeting of January 16, 1996. During the meeting the Presbytery arose as a Presbytery, was seated as a Corporation, elected officers, arose as a Corporation and was again seated as a Presbytery. The business transacted in that meeting is recorded within the minutes of Presbytery, not as separate minutes.

description: Expiration of license to preach. Then licentiate did not apply for renewal. Procedures for renewal not followed.
response: Presbytery met only two times in Stated Meetings in 1996: in January and again in October. In the interval between the meetings TE Ron Davis of Bethel Presbyterian Church in Lake Charles died. Licentiate RE Ben Thompson, a member of Bethel Presbyterian Church whose license was still valid, was asked by the Session of Bethel Presbyterian Church to fill the pulpit. Before the Stated meeting in October RE Thompson's license expired. When the Presbytery discovered the oversight in October 1996, the motion was made, citing BCO 19-2, to waive re-examination in this extraordinary case and to renew his license. RE Thompson had been preaching regularly under the supervision of the Bethel Session.

description: Session records not reviewed in 1996.
response: The Session Records Committee apparently did fail to examine minutes in 1996 due to the departure of the committee members from Presbytery. Subsequently, session records have been reviewed by the committee.

description: No standing rules submitted per RAO 14-4-c&e (second year).

description: The minutes of the called meeting do not state the purpose for the meeting.
Presbytery did not record why it considers a work a valid Christian ministry.

The report of the commission to install teaching elders contains no record of the following: 1) The pronouncement of installation (BCO 21-7); 2) The charge to the congregation (BCO 21-7); 3) That the TEs answered the constitutional questions in the affirmative (BCO 21-9); 4) That the congregation or session were asked the constitutional questions (BCO 21-10);
The Standing Rules mandate a meeting in October, yet no minutes for that meeting were submitted;
The Standing Rules are not complete.
No record of candidate questions put to transferring TE.
response: While presbytery did not specifically ask Rev. Amadeo Torres the questions put to candidates at the time of their ordination (BCO 21-5), the minutes of the Missouri Presbytery's Stated Meeting of 21 April 1998 clearly indicate that Rev. Torres signed his ministerial obligation which includes his written assent to all the ordination questions. Presbytery considered Rev. Torres' subscription to the Ministerial Obligation as fulfilling the requirement of BCO 13-6. However, to avoid confusion in the future, Missouri Presbytery will require a verbal response from each ordained minister seeking admission to the presbytery.

description: Allowed an unlicensed man to preach in a PCA pulpit for more than a year.
response: Our Candidates and Credentials Committee attempted to work with Westminster PC, Booneville, for about a year. At first the committee attempted to bring the retired Methodist minister that was filling the pulpit through the trials for ordination. When it was clear that he would not be able to pass the theological exams, they spoke with the elders of this small church (about 25 members) about their options. All of this took about a year and a half the matter has finally been resolved this past month when the church voted to pull out of the PCA.

description: the minutes of 1999 were submitted too late to be reviewed by the committee.
Churches not reporting to presbytery.
response: in the past two years all of our churches submitted their sessional records for review (reported). In 1998 Nashville Presbytery only met three times and not all the churches reported in that calendar year. We will take greater care to have our churches adhere to a schedule that meets the requirements of the BCO.

description: Standing rules on session records should comply with BCO 40-1,2,3.
response: nashville presbytery cannot detect the lack of compliance in our Standing Rules with BCO 40-1,2,3, nor has this been pointed out before. [Lengthy response excerpting Standing Rules chapters 3-1, 10-1, 10-2 and guidelines, with commitment to adopt compliant rules.]

description: No record of ordinands being asked if their views had changed since licensure.
response: Nashville Presbytery asks ordinands about changes in views as a regular part of their examination. The clerk has not recorded responses which reflect no change of views, but he will in the future.

description: Commissions minutes and reports not included.
response: Nashville Presbytery will take greater care to see that all commission reports and minutes are included with the minutes.

description: (p. 3): Items 75-17.f., 75-17.g., and 75-17.i, refer to appendices which are not attached.

Man licensed who "holds exception in his view of the Sabbath (he does not believe that the Sabbath Day and Lord's Day are the same), the law (he is not absolutely sure that the Decalogue is the New Testament Ethic), and on who may administer the Sacraments (he cannot see that the administration of the sacraments is the sole prerogative of teaching elders; he believes that ruling elders and others who have demonstrated knowledge, understanding, and maturity concerning the sacraments should be allowed to administer them.)"
response: That New River Presbytery resubmits its initial response and requests that the General Assembly accept it as satisfactory for the following reasons: Response: "Terry Roush has rescinded his exception concerning the Decalogue as the New Testament ethic and expressed a view consistent with the Reformed Faith. The Presbytery finds his other two exceptions allowable and therefore his license remains in effect." Reasons: 1. When the General Assembly acting at the 25th General Assembly, approved the original exception to our minutes, the Committee on Review of presbytery records had failed to report to the assembly that in the same motion in which the Presbytery approved the three original exceptions, it also approved the following statement: 'and that he be instructed not to preach or teach these exceptions in such a way to disturb the peace and purity of the church.' 2. Since the two remaining exceptions have not been found through required judicial and constitutional process to be considered as 'fundamentals of the system of doctrine' and thus are allowable exceptions, the Presbytery has the right to grant these exceptions, as outlined in BCO 39-3(1).

description: (page 6&7): No evidence that the TE was examined on Sacraments and Church Government.
(p. 8): AD HOC Committee, A TE cannot by himself ask a Shepherding Committee to visit. It requires request of a session.
(p. 5, #3): No evidence that TEs were examined in Theology.

Licentiate being examined for ordination was not examined in sacraments nor asked if his views have changed since licensure.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the error and will endeavor to guard against its occurrence in the future.

description: No quorum for called meeting. No ruling elder present.
response: The exception was holding called meetings without the presence of ruling elders. Our response to this is that it did not actually happen. The clerk reported the names of the churches represented, which were, in fact, represented by ruling elders. He simply neglected to record their names, not realizing the oversight since the church was listed as being represented. Presbytery regrets the omission.

description: No quorum for called meeting. No ruling elder present.
response: The exception was holding called meetings without the presence of ruling elders. Our response to this is that it did not actually happen. The omission is there, but so were the elders. The Clerk reported the names of the churches represented, which were, in fact, represented by ruling elders. He simply neglected to record their names, not realizing the oversight since the church was listed as being represented. Presbytery regrets the omission.

description: Licentiate being examined for ordination was not examined in sacraments nor asked if his views have changed since licensure.
response: Presbytery has instructed its Credentials Committee to prepare a checklist of those areas required by the Book of Church Order and that this checklist be utilized for Presbytery's examination of each licentiate.

description: Presbytery erred in allowing a congregation to dissolve a pastoral relationship.
response: presbytery respectfully suggests that a careful reading of the section of the minutes will disclose that the dissolution was accomplished by presbytery upon adoption of the appropriate presbytery Committee report.

description: Covenant questions should be addressed to the session, not the congregation in assistant pastor installation.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the error, and in the future will equip each ordination and/or installation with a checklist of procedures to insure that the Book of Church Order requirements are met.

description: Presbytery cannot restore from excommunication. This can only be done by session.
response: Presbytery calls attention to the fact that the individual who was restored was a former Teaching Elder who had been excommunicated by Presbytery and that the restoration was "through" the session of park view church.

description: no record of commissions provided.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the error and will diligently endeavor to require that commissions be presented and recorded at the time ordination and/or installations is approved.

description: No call presented, discussed or attached to be approved.
response: Presbytery acknowledges that the approved call was not attached as it should have been and will exercise care to insure that this error does not occur in the future.

description: Commission to receive a church did not have a quorum.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the error and will increase future commissions to three TE's and three RE's to insure a quorum of two TE's and RE's.

description: no record of call to TE and no record of installation commission.
response: Presbytery submits that the TE accepted a position with MTW and installation was not required.

description: no record that presbytery voted on any of the exceptions taken to sessional records or that any session has responded to exceptions taken to its records.

description: (#2206): Twelve month internship requirement reduced to nine months without explanation.
Presbytery erred in allowing commissions to take up matters at their own discretion.
response: RECOMMEND that the Presbytery rescind the action of the November, 1998 Pro re nata meeting and that the regional committees be instructed that they no longer have the authority to act as regional commissions except as specifically authorized by Presbytery for a particular matter. RECOMMEND that Presbytery direct the Stated Clerk to communicate this action to the General Assembly and to thank the Assembly for their oversight in this matter.

description: (p.2, Item 7): Minutes of called meeting January 23, 1999 are not included.
Presbytery erred in receiving a candidate who is not a member of the PCA.
response: Presbytery does not agree with the exception as stated. Simply put, the Presbytery never received the candidate in question, as stated in the exception. The Presbytery did not ever conceive of the possibility of waiving the requirement of BCO 18-2 that a candidate coming under care must be a member of a PCA church. The Presbytery assumed that by requiring an endorsement from a PCA body before a candidate could be received that such a candidate would be required (1) to be a communicant member of a PCA congregation for six months, and (2) to have a satisfactory endorsement presented from the governing body of that congregation. The Presbytery now realizes that in light of the wording of BCO 18-8 we should not have even proceeded with and sustained the first part of the examination for bringing a man under care. The Presbytery will not repeat such action in the future. We will not initiate any examinations of candidates for coming under care who are not first members of a PCA congregation.

description: No mention of men coming under care meeting the 6-month requirement.
response: The Presbytery of Northern Illinois regrets its error of omitting mention in its October 23, 1998, minutes of the fact that the men coming under care met the 6-month requirement of BCO 18-2. We have taken procedural steps that should prevent a recurrence of this omission.

1997 Minutes: Although 1997 minutes were submitted, the minutes did not contain a copy of the Directory or Standing Rules.
1997 Minutes not submitted for review.
response: The 1997 Minutes were submitted for review. [Minutes were turned in on May 1, 2000.]

response: The response was from the clerk, not from presbytery.

description: The Moderator appointed a commission without action of the Presbytery.
Installed a man who is not a pastor of a particular church.
Questions of a minister were asked, but not the questions of BCO.

response: Rocky Mountain Presbytery agrees with the exception and corrects its action and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: The proper procedure for the organization of a particular church was not followed.
response: Siouxlands Presbytery acknowledges and regrets the exception of substance to the Minutes of the September 24-25 Stated Meeting regarding failure to follow the proper procedure of the organization of particular church. Presbytery assures the General Assembly that the letter of petition from New Covenant Mission to become a particular church noted absent from the September 1998 Stated Meeting was received and acted upon at the January 1999 Stated Meeting: "MSO that we receive the petition of New Covenant PC to become a particular church. (Note 54-8: Upon receipt of petition, the MUM/MTW Committee recommends that Presbytery accept New Covenant's organizational service of August 30 as fulfilling BCO 5.8-11 requirements." Acknowledging our aforementioned error, Siouxlands Presbytery assures General Assembly of our intention to pursue the organization of future mission churches with great diligence.

description: (16.h): Theological paper approved in which candidate stated "I believe that God gives extraordinary gifts (miracles, healing, and tongues)".
BCO 13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause stated as rationale.
(11.f): BCO13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause found.

The worship service for the purpose of organizing a church and installing its pastor included a lady who "led one congregation in the opening prayer" and another lady who "read passages of Scripture" prior to the sermon.
response: Presbytery is sorry that exception occurred and will seek to prevent it from happening again.

description: (p.3, IX, B2): This is an exam for ordination not transfer. BCO 13-6 does not apply. Candidate should have been examined in Church History and English Bible.

(Minister and His Work Committee): Presbytery granted a TE permission to continue laboring out of bounds as a pastor of an independent reformed church in Michigan. 1) The record doesn't include a call or Presbytery's approval of a call, 2) There is no assurance that the TE will be allowed to teach and preach the whole council of God's word, and 3) There is no record that the TE had contacted Great Lakes Presbytery for approval to work within the bounds of that Presbytery.
(27502): Presbytery divested TE Wayne Fair without censure, but the record does not reflect his request for divestiture from the previous meeting.

No indication that candidate meets requirements of BCO 18-8, 18-2.
response: The candidate did meet the requirements of BCO 18-8, 18-2. However the clerk failed to record this fact in the Minutes.

description: No indication that candidate meets requirements of BCO 18-8, 18-2.
response: The candidate did meet the requirements of BCO 18-8, 18-2. However the clerk failed to record this fact in the Minutes.
